hints.Indicative: Indicative present would be 'facis' or 'facit' — 'fac' is the irregular imperative singular
hints.Subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'facias' — 'fac' is the imperative singular
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'facere' — 'fac' is the imperative singular

hints.ago, agere, egi, actum: These are the principal parts of 'agere' (to do/drive)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the principal parts of 'dare' (to give)
hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the principal parts of 'ducere' (to lead)

hints.Nominative: Nominative masculine would be 'salvus' — 'salvum' has the -um accusative ending agreeing with 'populum'
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'salvo' — 'salvum' has the -um accusative ending
hints.Vocative: Vocative would be 'salve' — 'salvum' is accusative agreeing with 'populum'

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'populus' is 'populus' — 'populum' has the -um accusative ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'populus' would be 'populi' — 'populum' has the -um accusative ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'populus' would be 'populo' — 'populum' has the -um accusative ending

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'populi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'populi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'populi' ends in -i

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'Dominus' — 'Domine' has the -e vocative ending of 2nd declension
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Dominus' is 'Domini' — 'Domine' is the vocative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Dominus' is 'Dominum' — 'Domine' is the vocative form
